nodes.name: Distributed Attacks in Persistent-State AI Control
nodes.description: As AI coding agents become more autonomous, they increasingly ship code iteratively, with the codebase persisting across sessions. This persistence creates a new attack surface: a misaligned or prompt

nodes.name: LACUNA: A Testbed for Evaluating Localization Precision for LLM Unlearning
nodes.description: LLMs memorize sensitive training data, including personally identifiable information (PII), creating a pressing need for reliable post hoc removal methods. Unlearning has emerged as a promising soluti

nodes.name: Program-as-Weights: A Programming Paradigm for Fuzzy Functions
nodes.description: Many everyday programming tasks resist clean rule-based implementation, such as alerting on important log lines, repairing malformed JSON, or ranking search results by intent, and are increasingly out

nodes.name: Online Safety Monitoring for LLMs
nodes.description: Despite alignment training, LLMs remain prone to generating unsafe outputs at deployment time. Monitoring outputs online and raising an alarm when safety can no longer be assumed is therefore critical

nodes.name: ReContext: Recursive Evidence Replay as LLM Harness for Long-Context Reasoning
nodes.description: Understanding and reasoning over long contexts has become a key requirement for deploying large language models (LLMs) in realistic applications. Although recent LLMs support increasingly long context

nodes.name: What LLM Agents Say When No One Is Watching: Social Structure and Latent Objective Emergence in Multi-Agent Debates
nodes.description: LLM agents will increasingly act in socially structured settings where role, audience, and relational context can shape what is advantageous or costly to say. We study whether such social structure, w

nodes.name: Reasoning LLM Improves Speaker Recognition in Long-form TV Dramas
nodes.description: Long-form TV dramas present a formidable challenge for comprehensive video understanding, where deciphering complex storyline often relies on \textbf{speaker recognition}, the task of accurately attri

nodes.name: DemoPSD: Disagreement-Modulated Policy Self-Distillation
nodes.description: On-policy self-distillation (OPSD) has emerged as a practical method for training large language models (LLMs) to reason, where a single model acts as both the teacher and the student with different l

nodes.name: Beyond Adam: SOAP and Muon for Faster, Label-Efficient Training of Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
nodes.description: Machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) have become a hallmark of AI for scientific simulation. While efforts on new architectures and datasets have led to increasingly accurate and general mo

nodes.name: Controllable Sim Agents with Behavior Latents
nodes.description: Realistic traffic simulation requires agents that imitate logged behavior and can also be steered along interpretable axes. Such controllability enables engineers to isolate variables, reproduce speci

nodes.name: Mem0
nodes.description: Production-ready long-term memory for AI agents. Supports graph memory, personalized memory, and cross-session persistence.

nodes.name: Zep
nodes.description: Temporal memory framework for conversational agents. Focuses on fact extraction, content summarization, and temporal reasoning.

nodes.name: LangMem
nodes.description: LangChain native memory components for agents. Supports event memory, fact/preference memory, and procedural memory with hot-path and background updates.

nodes.name: Letta
nodes.description: Long-running agent framework with persistent state management. Designed for ultra-long-running agents.

nodes.name: Cognee
nodes.description: Knowledge graph-based memory. Structures memory as a knowledge graph for complex relationship reasoning.

nodes.name: Short-term Memory
nodes.description: Context window and working memory for the current task. Limited by model context length.

nodes.name: Long-term Memory
nodes.description: Persistent memory that survives across sessions and tasks. Stored in external databases.

nodes.name: Episodic Memory
nodes.description: Memory of specific past experiences and interactions. Records what happened, when, and where.

nodes.name: Semantic Memory
nodes.description: Factual knowledge and concept relationships. General world knowledge independent of specific episodes.

nodes.name: Procedural Memory
nodes.description: Memory of how to perform tasks and procedures. Encodes skills and step-by-step processes.

nodes.name: Graph Memory
nodes.description: Knowledge graph-based memory structure. Stores entities and relationships as an interconnected graph.

nodes.name: langchain-ai/langgraph
nodes.description: Build resilient agents.

nodes.name: crewAIInc/crewAI
nodes.description: Framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents. By fostering collaborative intelligence, CrewAI empowers agents to work together seamlessly, tackling complex tasks.

nodes.name: microsoft/autogen
nodes.description: A programming framework for agentic AI

nodes.name: run-llama/llama_index
nodes.description: LlamaIndex is the leading document agent and OCR platform

nodes.name: microsoft/semantic-kernel
nodes.description: Integrate cutting-edge LLM technology quickly and easily into your apps

nodes.name: langgenius/dify
nodes.description: Production-ready platform for agentic workflow development.

nodes.name: openai/swarm
nodes.description: Educational framework exploring ergonomic, lightweight multi-agent orchestration. Managed by OpenAI Solution team.

nodes.name: mem0ai/mem0
nodes.description: Universal memory layer for AI Agents

nodes.name: getzep/zep
nodes.description: Zep | Examples, Integrations, & More

nodes.name: letta-ai/letta
nodes.description: Platform for stateful agents: AI with advanced memory that can learn and self-improve over time.

nodes.name: topoteretes/cognee
nodes.description: Cognee is the open-source AI memory platform for agents. Give your AI agents persistent long-term memory across sessions with a self-hosted knowledge graph engine.

nodes.name: IterativeVibeCoding
nodes.description: A setting for AI control where a coding agent builds software over a sequence of pull requests in a persistent codebase while pursuing a covert side task.

nodes.name: StatefulLinkTracker
nodes.description: A monitor that tracks suspicious buildup across pull requests to detect gradual attacks.

nodes.name: TrajectoryMonitor
nodes.description: A monitor that evaluates the sequence of actions or steps taken by an agent.

nodes.name: DiffMonitor
nodes.description: A monitor that evaluates code changes by looking at the accumulated history of diffs.

nodes.name: MonitorEnsemble
nodes.description: A defensive strategy combining multiple monitors, such as trajectory and diff monitors, to reduce attack evasion.

nodes.name: MachineUnlearning
nodes.description: A technique used to remove specific sensitive training data or knowledge from a model's parameters.

nodes.name: LocalizeFirstUnlearnSecond
nodes.description: A paradigm that first identifies the specific model parameters responsible for storing targeted knowledge before applying unlearning methods to them.

nodes.name: Lacuna
nodes.description: An unlearning testbed framework that provides ground-truth parameter-level localization to evaluate if unlearning truly erases knowledge.

nodes.name: MaskedContinualPretraining
nodes.description: A method used to inject specific synthetic information into predefined parameters of a model for evaluation purposes.

nodes.name: GradientBasedUnlearning
nodes.description: A technique that utilizes gradients to effectively erase specific knowledge from model weights when localization is successful.

nodes.name: FuzzyFunctionProgramming
nodes.description: A programming paradigm that compiles a function from a natural-language specification into a compact, locally-executable neural artifact.

nodes.name: ProgramAsWeights
nodes.description: A paradigm where a compiler emits parameter-efficient adapters for a frozen, lightweight interpreter to execute programs.

nodes.name: DirectPrompting
nodes.description: A technique where a model is directly prompted to solve a problem for each input.

nodes.name: RiskControl
nodes.description: A statistical framework used to calibrate a threshold for turning a verifier signal into an alarm decision.

nodes.name: SequentialHypothesisTesting
nodes.description: A statistical method used as a basis for advanced monitoring of model outputs over time.

nodes.name: ReContext
nodes.description: A training-free inference method that uses model-internal relevance signals to construct a query-conditioned evidence pool and replays it before final generation to improve long-context reasoning.
